text:
Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, often used in software development. It focuses on iterative development, where work is divided into short cycles called sprints. Each sprint typically lasts a few weeks and ends with a working product increment.

Teams hold daily standups—brief meetings where members discuss what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles. Before each sprint, the team selects tasks from the backlog, a prioritized list of user stories. User stories are simple descriptions of a feature from an end user's perspective.

By repeating these cycles, agile allows teams to adapt quickly to change and deliver value to customers steadily.
